exam_price_notes: ~€160–€200 (~$175–$220 USD) depending on country and centre; highest DELF/DALF fee

testing_centers.provider: Institut Français, Alliance Française, and authorised exam centres (175 countries)

id_requirements: Valid government-issued photo ID required (passport, national identity card, or equivalent). Name on registration must exactly match ID presented on exam day.
exam_rules: No dictionaries, notes, or electronic devices permitted during written components. Oral components are conducted with an examiner (face-to-face or recorded). Candidates must arrive at least 30 minutes before the scheduled start time. Written and oral components may be on different days depending on the centre.

prerequisites.description: None; designed for near-native or highly advanced speakers; recommended DALF C1 level or equivalent professional/academic French experience

retake_policy.notes: No mandatory waiting period between DELF/DALF attempts. Candidates may retake any diploma level at any available session. Exam sessions vary by country — typically 2–4 per year depending on the centre.

domains.name: Compréhension et production orales — Oral Comprehension and Production
Compréhension et production écrites — Written Comprehension and Production
